caveats: to-local offsets are SYSTEMATICS-DOMINATED (sample selection; Ubler note the z=0->high-z evolution can be 'insignificant' if disturbed/dispersion-dominated galaxies are included); stat errors below are lower bounds on the true error.
the INTERNAL z~0.9->z~2.3 change is the robust, systematics-controlled quantity.

MASS BIAS: KMOS3D is massive disks (log M* > ~10.5), HIGH surface brightness, i.e. the HIGH-acceleration (Newtonian) regime -- where the DEV a0(z) signal is intrinsically weak. The DEV/SPARC calibration is LOW-mass, deep-MOND galaxies.
